Mangosteen (Garcinia mangostana) is a tropical fruit-bearing tree native to Southeast Asia, known for its soft, white interior and purple exterior. The fruit and leaves of the mangosteen tree have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of mangosteen include xanthones, such as alpha-mangostin and gamma-mangostin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anticancer effects. Mangosteen is commonly used in herbal preparations, including capsules, teas, and juices, which are marketed for their potential health benefits, including weight loss, immune system support, and skin health improvement.
